assumptions: No Better Stack account, team membership, monitor, alert channel, escalation policy, or prior configuration is assumed.
A browser, email address, reachable URL or IP address, and network connection are available.
No email was submitted, magic link was requested or opened, account or team was created or joined, or monitor was created during research.

developer_goal: Start without a Better Stack account, create the first HTTP status monitor for a URL, retain the basic single-user escalation behavior where applicable, and verify that the monitor is running and continuously checking the URL.

first_success.outcome: A Better Stack Uptime monitor is running and continuously checking the supplied URL or IP address for an HTTP 2XX success response.
first_success.observable_signal: After Create monitor is clicked, the official guide states that the monitor is up and running and continuously checking the URL.

route_selection.surface: Better Stack Uptime first HTTP monitor
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Better Stack Uptime first HTTP monitor
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Tracing
candidates.reason: This is a separate product surface with application instrumentation and language-specific setup.

candidates.name: Log management
candidates.reason: This requires selecting and configuring a log source rather than following the named first-monitor path.

candidates.name: Infrastructure monitoring
candidates.reason: This requires host or cluster instrumentation and is not the selected first-monitor guide.

required_path.action: Complete Better Stack account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Better Stack account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Better Stack candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: After Create monitor is clicked, the official guide states that the monitor is up and running and continuously checking the URL.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'Provide an email address and receive a Better Stack magic link.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Join an existing team associated with the email domain or create a new account when that branch is offered.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'environment', 'requirement': 'Have a URL or IP address that Better Stack can check for an HTTP 2XX response.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: The documentation home presents multiple peer product surfaces without selecting one universal first value outcome.
A Better Stack account or existing-team membership is required before the monitor form can be used.
Signup uses an email magic link, but its post-submit and verification states are not publicly documented.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Create the first HTTP status monitor.

excluded.item: Bulk importing monitors, adding advanced monitor types, configuring status pages, instrumenting traces or logs, and broader incident-management work occur after the first monitor is running.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Get started with monitoring | Better Stack Documentation
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Sign up | Better Stack

uncertainties.question: Which Better Stack product surface should define platform-wide first success?
uncertainties.impact: The prerequisites, route, and completion signal differ across Uptime, incidents and on-call, status pages, infrastructure monitoring, logs, tracing, error tracking, and AI SRE.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The official documentation home presents these as peer surfaces and does not select one universal first task.

uncertainties.question: What exact states follow Send me a magic link for a new user?
uncertainties.impact: The mandatory signup and product-entry segment cannot be recorded atomically.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The public signup page shows the initial controls but the official docs do not enumerate delivery, verification, profile, team, and Uptime convergence states.

uncertainties.question: Which alert method is preselected for a new single-user account?
uncertainties.impact: The exact monitor-form click path may vary.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The guide lists alerting options and a default escalation behavior but does not identify a universal alert-channel default.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Better Stack show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
